question.text: Seema invests Rs. 50,000 at the beginning of each year for 5 years at 10% p.a. The future value of her investments at the end of 5 years is closest to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FV of annuity due = PMT × [((1+r)^n - 1) / r] × (1+r) = 50,000 × [(1.1^5 - 1)/0.1] × 1.1 = 50,000 × 6.1051 × 1.1 = 50,000 × 6.7156 = Rs. 3,35,781. Closest to Rs. 3,36,255. **The Trap:** Rs. 3,05,255 is the ordinary annuity FV (without the × 1.1 factor). Since investments are at the beginning of each year, it's annuity due. **Cross-Exam Context:** Annuity due calculations are tested in X-A (Ch 2) and XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SIPs at the beginning of the month vs end of month make a meaningful difference over long periods — always recommend early investment.
